title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in the computer. If they are, then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

The dataset contains year-end examination grades of a cohort of Primary 3 students from a local school. It has seven attributes — four categorical (ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE) and three continuous (MATHS, ENGLISH, SCIENCE).

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in the computer. If they are, then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

The dataset contains year-end examination grades of a cohort of Primary 3 students from a local school. It has seven attributes — four categorical (ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE) and three continuous (MATHS, ENGLISH, SCIENCE).

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Bar Chart — geom_bar()
A bar chart showing the count of students by race.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) +
  geom_bar()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Dot Plot — geom_dotplot()
A dot plot of Maths scores, where each dot represents one observation.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot(binwidth = 2.5,
               dotsize = 0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Histogram — geom_histogram()
A histogram of Maths scores coloured by gender.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20,
                 color = "grey30")

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Kernel Density Estimate — geom_density()
A kernel density plot comparing Maths score distributions between genders.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           colour = GENDER)) +
  geom_density()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Box Plot — geom_boxplot()
A notched box plot of Maths scores by gender. Non-overlapping notches indicate differing medians.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot(notch = TRUE)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Violin Plot — geom_violin()
A violin plot comparing the full distribution shape of Maths scores by gender.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_violin()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Combining Geoms — Box Plot + Jitter
Data points overlaid on box plots using geom_point() with jitter.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(position = "jitter",
             size = 0.5)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Adding Mean Points — stat_summary()
Mean values added to box plots using stat_summary().

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(geom = "point",
               fun = "mean",
               colour = "red",
               size = 4)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Best Fit Line — geom_smooth()
A scatter plot of Maths vs English with a linear regression line.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = lm,
              linewidth = 0.5)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Trellis Plot — facet_wrap()
Histograms of Maths scores faceted by class.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) +
  facet_wrap(~ CLASS)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Flipped Bar Chart — coord_flip()
A horizontal bar chart of race counts with theme_minimal().

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1: A Layered Grammar of Graphics
text:
Fixed Axis Range — coord_cartesian()
Scatter plot with both axes fixed from 0 to 100.

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = lm,
              linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100),
                  ylim = c(0, 100))

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
pacman::p_load(ggiraph, plotly, patchwork, DT, tidyverse,
               readxl, gifski, gapminder, gganimate)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
pacman::p_load(ggiraph, plotly, patchwork, DT, tidyverse,
               readxl, gifski, gapminder, gganimate)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
Interactive Data Visualisation: ggiraph

Tooltip effect

The PlotThe Code











p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618)






Displaying multiple information on tooltip

The PlotThe Code











exam_data$tooltip <- c(paste0("Name = ", exam_data$ID,
                               "\n Class = ", exam_data$CLASS))

p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = exam_data$tooltip),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618)






Customising Tooltip style

The PlotThe Code











tooltip_css <- "background-color:white; font-style:bold; color:black;"

p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p,
       width_svg = 6,
       height_svg = 6*0.618,
       options = list(opts_tooltip(css = tooltip_css)))






Displaying statistics on tooltip

The PlotThe Code











tooltip <- function(y, ymax, accuracy = .01) {
  mean <- scales::number(y, accuracy = accuracy)
  sem <- scales::number(ymax - y, accuracy = accuracy)
  paste("Mean maths scores:", mean, "+/-", sem)
}

gg_point <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = RACE)) +
  stat_summary(aes(y = MATHS,
                   tooltip = after_stat(tooltip(y, ymax))),
               fun.data = "mean_se",
               geom = GeomInteractiveCol,
               fill = "light blue") +
  stat_summary(aes(y = MATHS),
               fun.data = mean_se,
               geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 0.2)

girafe(ggobj = gg_point, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618)






Hover effect with data_id

The PlotThe Code











p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618)






Styling hover effect

The PlotThe Code











p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p,
       width_svg = 6,
       height_svg = 6*0.618,
       options = list(
         opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
         opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")))






Combining tooltip and hover effect

The PlotThe Code











p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(tooltip = CLASS, data_id = CLASS),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p,
       width_svg = 6,
       height_svg = 6*0.618,
       options = list(
         opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
         opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")))






Click effect with onclick

The PlotThe Code











exam_data$onclick <- sprintf("window.open(\"%s%s\")",
  "https://www.moe.gov.sg/schoolfinder?journey=Primary%20school",
  as.character(exam_data$ID))

p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(onclick = onclick),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618)






Coordinated Multiple Views with ggiraph

The PlotThe Code











p1 <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

p2 <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_dotplot_interactive(
    aes(data_id = ID),
    stackgroups = TRUE,
    binwidth = 1,
    method = "histodot") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL)

girafe(code = print(p1 + p2),
       width_svg = 6,
       height_svg = 3,
       options = list(
         opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"),
         opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;")))

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
Interactive Data Visualisation: plotly

Interactive scatter plot: plot_ly()

The PlotThe Code











plot_ly(data = exam_data,
        x = ~MATHS,
        y = ~ENGLISH,
        color = ~RACE)






Interactive scatter plot: ggplotly()

The PlotThe Code











p <- ggplot(data = exam_data,
            aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH, color = RACE)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 100))

ggplotly(p)






Coordinated Multiple Views with plotly

The PlotThe Code











d <- highlight_key(exam_data)

p1 <- ggplot(data = d, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 100))

p2 <- ggplot(data = d, aes(x = ENGLISH, y = SCIENCE)) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 100), ylim = c(0, 100))

subplot(ggplotly(p1), ggplotly(p2))

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
Interactive Data Table: DT

DT::datatable(exam_data, class = "compact")

title: Hands-on Exercise 3
text:
Animated Data Visualisation: gganimate

Import Global Population data

col <- c("Country", "Continent")
globalPop <- read_xls("data/GlobalPopulation.xls", sheet = "Data") %>%
  mutate(across(col, as.factor)) %>%
  mutate(Year = as.integer(Year))



Static bubble plot

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(globalPop, aes(x = Old, y = Young,
                      size = Population,
                      colour = Country)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = country_colors) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +
  labs(title = 'Year: {frame_time}',
       x = '% Aged', y = '% Young')






Animated bubble plot: gganimate

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(globalPop, aes(x = Old, y = Young,
                      size = Population,
                      colour = Country)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = country_colors) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +
  labs(title = 'Year: {frame_time}',
       x = '% Aged', y = '% Young') +
  transition_time(Year) +
  ease_aes('linear')






Animated bubble plot: ggplotly()

The PlotThe Code











gg <- ggplot(globalPop, aes(x = Old, y = Young,
                             size = Population,
                             colour = Country,
                             frame = Year)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = country_colors) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 12)) +
  labs(x = '% Aged', y = '% Young') +
  theme(legend.position = 'none')

ggplotly(gg)






Animated bubble plot: plot_ly()

The PlotThe Code











bp <- globalPop %>%
  plot_ly(x = ~Old,
          y = ~Young,
          size = ~Population,
          color = ~Continent,
          frame = ~Year,
          text = ~Country,
          hoverinfo = "text",
          type = 'scatter',
          mode = 'markers') %>%
  layout(showlegend = FALSE)

bp

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
pacman::p_load(ggrepel, patchwork, ggthemes, hrbrthemes, tidyverse)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
pacman::p_load(ggrepel, patchwork, ggthemes, hrbrthemes, tidyverse)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
Beyond ggplot2 Annotation: ggrepel

Without ggrepel

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text(aes(label = ID), hjust = 0.5, vjust = -0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores - without ggrepel")






With ggrepel

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = ID), size = 3) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores - with ggrepel")

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
Beyond ggplot2 Themes

ggthemes — Economist theme

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_economist()






hrbrthemes

The PlotThe Code














ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_ipsum(axis_title_size = 18, base_size = 15, grid = "Y")

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
Beyond Single Graph: patchwork

Two graphs side by side

The PlotThe Code














p1 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "grey30", fill = "grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores")

p2 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, color = "grey30", fill = "grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of English scores")

p1 + p2






Three graphs combined

The PlotThe Code














p3 <- ggplot(exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = lm, linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100), ylim = c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English vs Maths scores")

(p1 / p2) | p3






With auto-tagging

The PlotThe Code














((p1 / p2) | p3) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'I')






Inset plot

The PlotThe Code














p3 + inset_element(p2, left = 0.02, bottom = 0.7, right = 0.5, top = 1)

title: ISSS608-VAA
text: Welcome to ISSS608 Visual Analytics and Applications. In this website, you will find my coursework prepared for this course.
